summary: Completed frontend testing of page transition animations implemented using framer-motion. All animations working correctly across Home, Explore, Reels, Messages/Chat, and Settings pages. Navigation via sidebar triggers smooth fade-in/out and y-transform transitions as specified.

issues: WebSocket connection warning appears on page load (non-blocking): 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'

passed_tests: Auth page loads with fade-in animation
Home page loads with AnimatedPage wrapper applying pageVariants
Navigation from Home to Explore triggers exit/enter animations
Navigation from Explore to Reels triggers smooth transition
Navigation from Reels to Messages/Chat triggers smooth transition
Navigation to Settings page via URL triggers enter animation
Navigation from Settings back to Home triggers exit/enter animations
AnimatePresence mode='wait' waits for exit animation before entering new page
location.pathname used as key ensures unique animation per route
pageVariants defines correct initial (opacity:0, y:20), enter (opacity:1, y:0), exit (opacity:0, y:-10) states
Sidebar navigation active state updates correctly after page transition
No content flashing observed during transitions
critical_code_review_comments: Animation implementation is correct: AnimatePresence with mode='wait' in AppRoutes
AnimatedPage wrapper properly applies pageVariants to each route
pageVariants in /components/animations/index.jsx defines smooth ease curves [0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94]
Route components (Home, Explore, Settings, etc.) render inside AnimatedPage wrapper
AnimatePresence initial={false} prevents animation on first mount - this matches expected behavior
seed_data_creation: Created test user animtest001@example.com via API
context_for_next_testing_agent: Page transition animations implemented and working. Uses framer-motion AnimatePresence with mode='wait' and AnimatedPage wrapper. All routes wrapped with AnimatedPage component in App.js AppRoutes function. WebSocket connection warning is a known non-blocking issue from previous iterations.
